items.title: Complete
items.contentHtml:
Phases of the cell cycle

Phases of the cell cycle

  • G1: cell growth, synthesis of RNA and proteins; variable duration (hours to days).
  • S: DNA synthesis (semi-conservative replication) and centriole duplication.
  • G2: preparation for mitosis, synthesis of microtubules and spindle proteins.
  • M: mitosis (prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase) and cytokinesis.
  • [Trap exam]: Phase S is the only one where DNA is duplicated; G2 does not replicate DNA.


Cycle checkpoints

Checkpoints

  • G1/S (restriction point): evaluates cell size, nutrients, DNA damage; if damage → arrest mediated by p53/p21.
  • G2/M: verifies integrity of the replicated DNA and completeness of replication; arrest by Chk1/Chk2.
  • M (spindle checkpoint): ensures correct attachment of chromosomes to the spindle in metaphase; defects activate BubR1 and Mad2 → blockade of APC/C.


Regulation by cyclin-CDK

Cyclin-CDK

  • Key complexes: Cyclin D/CDK4/6 (G1), Cyclin E/CDK2 (G1/S transition), Cyclin A/CDK2 (S), Cyclin B/CDK1 (G2/M).
  • Mechanism: cyclin binds to CDK → activating phosphorylation (CAK) and dephosphorylation (Cdc25) → activation.
  • Inhibitors: p21 (universal), p16 (CDK4/6 specific); both are tumor suppressors.


p53 and G1/S arrest

p53

  • Function: transcription factor that is stabilized by DNA damage (phosphorylation by ATM/ATR).
  • Effect: induces transcription of p21 → inhibits CDK2 and CDK4/6 → arrest in G1/S.
  • Pearl examination: p53 mutation is the most frequent in cancer (>50%); allows progression even with genomic damage.
  • Trap: p53 also activates apoptosis through the intrinsic pathway (Bax, Puma).


Rb and transcriptional control

Retinoblastoma (Rb)

  • Mechanism: Hypophosphorylated Rb binds to E2F → represses transcription of genes necessary for phase S.
  • Phosphorylation: by Cyclin D/CDK4/6 in G1 → releases E2F → transcriptional activation.
  • Alteration: loss of Rb (mutation or inactivation by HPV E7) → uncontrolled proliferation.
  • Pearl: Rb is the prototype of gatekeeper tumor.


Separase and anaphase

Anaphase and separase

  • Cohesin: protein complex that keeps sister chromatids together from S phase.
  • Separase: protease that cleaves the Scc1 subunit of cohesin → separation of chromatids.
  • Regulation: separase is inhibited by securin; APC/C activated by Cdc20 ubiquitin securin → degradation → separase activation.
  • [Key finding]: separase failure → aneuploidy and cancer.


Apoptosis due to signal deprivation

Apoptosis due to lack of signals

  • Pathway extrinsic: death receptors (Fas, TNF) activate caspase 8.
  • Intrinsic (mitochondrial) pathway: lack of growth factors → Bcl-2/Bax imbalance → release of cytochrome c → caspase 9.
  • Pearl: cells that lose survival signals enter anoikis (apoptosis due to matrix detachment).
  • Trap: apoptosis is not inflammatory (unlike necrosis).


State G0

G0: quiescence and senescence

  • Quiescence: reversible; Cells can reenter G1 when faced with stimuli (e.g. hepatocytes after hepatectomy).
  • Senescence: irreversible; permanent arrest due to telomere shortening or cellular damage; secretion of SASP (senescence-associated secretory phenotype).
  • Marker: Senescence-associated β-galactosidase (SA-β-gal) at pH 6.


S phase: replication and centrioles

S phase

  • DNA synthesis: semiconservative replication; replication forks with DNA polymerase δ/ε.
  • Duplication of centrioles: each parent centriole serves as a template for a daughter centriole (semi-conservative process).
  • Control: Cyclin A/CDK2 regulates progression in S; inhibition by p27.
  • [Key finding]: S cells are sensitive to radiation and chemotherapy (antimetabolites).


Mitosis promoting factor (MPF)

Cyclin B/CDK1 (MPF)

  • Composition: Cyclin B + CDK1 (Cdc2) spindle.
  • Inactivation: degradation of cyclin B by APC/C at the end of mitosis.

items.foco: G1, S, G2 and M phases of the cell cycle
items.title: Cell cycle phases
items.contentHtml:
Phases of the cell cycle

  • G1: cell growth, RNA and protein synthesis; variable duration (hours to days).
  • S: DNA synthesis (semi-conservative replication) and centriole duplication.
  • G2: preparation for mitosis, synthesis of microtubules and spindle proteins.
  • M: mitosis (prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase) and cytokinesis.
  • [Trap exam]: Phase S is the only one where DNA is duplicated; G2 does not replicate DNA.

items.foco: Checkpoints in G1/S, G2/M and M
items.title: Cycle checkpoints
items.contentHtml:
Control points

  • G1/S (restriction point): evaluates cell size, nutrients, DNA damage; if damage → arrest mediated by p53/p21.
  • G2/M: verifies integrity of the replicated DNA and completeness of replication; arrest by Chk1/Chk2.
  • M (spindle checkpoint): ensures correct attachment of chromosomes to the spindle in metaphase; defects activate BubR1 and Mad2 → blockade of APC/C.

items.contentHtml:

  • Key complexes: Cyclin D/CDK4/6 (G1), Cyclin E/CDK2 (G1/S transition), Cyclin A/CDK2 (S), Cyclin B/CDK1 (G2/M).
  • Mechanism: cyclin binds to CDK → activating phosphorylation (CAK) and dephosphorylation (Cdc25) → activation.
  • Inhibitors: p21 (universal), p16 (CDK4/6 specific); both are tumor suppressors.

items.foco: p53 stops the cycle in G1/S due to DNA damage
items.title: p53 and arrest in G1/S
items.contentHtml:
p53

  • Function: transcription factor that is stabilized by DNA damage (phosphorylation by ATM/ATR).
  • Effect: induces transcription of p21 → inhibits CDK2 and CDK4/6 → arrest in G1/S.
  • Pearl examination: p53 mutation is the most frequent in cancer (>50%); allows progression even with genomic damage.
  • Trap: p53 also activates apoptosis through the intrinsic pathway (Bax, Puma).

items.title: Rb and transcriptional control
items.contentHtml:
Retinoblastoma (Rb)

  • Mechanism: Hypophosphorylated Rb binds to E2F → represses transcription of genes necessary for S phase.
  • Phosphorylation: by Cyclin D/CDK4/6 in G1 → releases E2F → transcriptional activation.
  • Alteration: loss of Rb (mutation or inactivation by HPV E7) → uncontrolled proliferation.
  • Pearl: Rb is the prototype of tumor gatekeeper.

items.foco: Anaphase promoted by separase after cohesion
items.title: Separase and anaphase
items.contentHtml:
Anaphase and separase

  • Cohesin: protein complex that holds sister chromatids together from S phase.
  • Separase: protease that cleaves the Scc1 subunit of cohesin → chromatid separation.
  • Regulation: separase is inhibited by securin; APC/C activated by Cdc20 ubiquitin securin → degradation → separase activation.
  • [Key finding]: separase failure → aneuploidy and cancer.

items.foco: Apoptosis due to lack of survival signals
items.title: Apoptosis due to signal deprivation
items.contentHtml:
Apoptosis due to lack of signals

  • Extrinsic pathway: death receptors (Fas, TNF) activate caspase 8.
  • Intrinsic pathway (mitochondrial): lack of growth factors → Bcl-2/Bax imbalance → release of cytochrome c → caspase 9.
  • Pearl: cells that lose survival signals enter anoikis (apoptosis due to matrix detachment).
  • Trap: apoptosis is not inflammatory (unlike necrosis).

items.foco: Cells in G0 are quiescent or senescent
items.title: G0 State
items.contentHtml:
G0: quiescence and senescence

  • Quiescence: reversible; Cells can reenter G1 when faced with stimuli (e.g. hepatocytes after hepatectomy).
  • Senescence: irreversible; permanent arrest due to telomere shortening or cellular damage; secretion of SASP (senescence-associated secretory phenotype).
  • Marker: senescence-associated β-galactosidase (SA-β-gal) at pH 6.

items.foco: S phase DNA synthesis and centriole duplication
items.title: S phase: replication and centrioles
items.contentHtml:
S phase

  • DNA synthesis: semiconservative replication; replication forks with DNA polymerase δ/ε.
  • Duplication of centrioles: each parent centriole serves as a template for a daughter centriole (semi-conservative process).
  • Control: Cyclin A/CDK2 regulates progression in S; inhibition by p27.
  • [Key finding]: cells in S are sensitive to radiation and chemotherapy (antimetabolites).

items.foco: Cyclin B/CDK1 is the mitosis-promoting factor
items.title: Mitosis promoting factor (MPF)
items.contentHtml:
Cyclin B/CDK1 (MPF)

  • Composition: Cyclin B + CDK1 (Cdc2).
  • Activation: phosphorylation at Thr161 by CAK and dephosphorylation at Thr14/Tyr15 by Cdc25.
  • Function: phosphorylates lamins, histone H1, and spindle proteins → chromosome condensation, nuclear envelope breakdown and spindle formation.
  • Inactivation: degradation of cyclin B by APC/C at the end of mitosis.
